# Test Review — ai_chat/ui/controllers

**Scope:** `lib/features/ai_chat/ui/controllers/` + `test/features/ai_chat/ui/controllers/`
**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

## File summary

| File | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `chat_recorder_controller.dart` | 806 | Yes (3340 ln) | Oversized impl; inline mock classes in test; GetIt pattern diverges from `setUpTestGetIt()` |
| `chat_session_controller.dart` | 428 | Yes (1492 ln) | `Future.delayed(Duration.zero)` used multiple times (fake-time violation) |

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_recorder_controller.dart` at 806 lines is the most oversized file in the scope. Natural split seams:
  - `ChatRecorderState` + `ChatRecorderConfig` + `ChatRecorderErrorType` + `_AppLifecycleObserver` → `chat_recorder_state.dart` (~80 ln)
  - `ChatRecorderController` batch-recording path (`start`, `stopAndTranscribe`, `_transcribe`, `_cleanupInternal`, amplitude subscription) → `chat_recorder_controller.dart` (~450 ln)
  - `ChatRecorderController` realtime path (`startRealtime`, `stopRealtime`) → `chat_realtime_recorder_controller.dart` or a mixin/extension (~250 ln)
  - The test file at 3340 lines should mirror the split; realtime group tests naturally form a second file.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_session_controller_test.dart` at 1492 lines has naturally grouped sections (streaming, error, flush, finalize edge cases). Splitting at `sendMessage` streaming/flush group versus basic CRUD group would yield two ~700-line files without losing coverage.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_sessions_controller.dart` (200 ln) and `chat_stream_utils.dart` (198 ln) are within acceptable range.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_recorder_controller_test.dart` lines 29–113: four inline mock/helper classes (`_MockAudioRecorder`, `_MockCloudInferenceRepository`, `_MockTranscriptionService`, `_MockRealtimeService`, `_InMemoryAiConfigRepo`) are defined in the test file. Per guidelines, mocks that exist or could exist in `test/mocks/mocks.dart` must live there. Add them centrally and import instead.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_recorder_controller_test.dart` lines 155–168: manual `getIt.isRegistered` / `getIt.unregister` / `getIt.registerSingleton` inline boilerplate rather than `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()`. Standardise to the shared helpers from `test/widget_test_utils.dart`.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_sessions_controller_test.dart` line 64: `await Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` is a real-time wait inside a unit test — violates Fake Time Policy. Replace with `await pumpEventQueue()` or drive the provider's async `build` call explicitly.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_session_controller_test.dart` (lines ~793, 857, 927, 989, 1059, 1143, 1213): `await Future<void>.delayed(Duration.zero)` is used throughout the streaming tests to yield control. This is a real-timer micro-yield. While `Duration.zero` is low-risk, it is explicitly listed in the guidelines as something to replace with `async.flushMicrotasks()` (unit tests) or `await tester.pump()` (widget tests). Since these are unit tests using a `ProviderContainer`, the correct replacement is `await pumpEventQueue()` or `async.flushMicrotasks()` inside `fakeAsync`. Most of these tests are good candidates for wrapping in `fakeAsync`.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_recorder_controller_test.dart` lines 343–420 ("dispose cleans files"): the test's "pump 10 times" loop (`for (var i = 0; i < 10; i++) { await pumpEventQueue(); ... }`) is a polling loop using real I/O. The `onDispose` cleanup is `unawaited` by design; a more deterministic approach would be to expose a `@visibleForTesting` cleanup completer, or use the existing `_MockTranscriptionService` chain to synchronise. The pattern is repeated at line 960 and should be extracted to a shared helper.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_session_controller_test.dart`: the mock stub block `when(() => mockChatRepository.createSession(...)).thenAnswer(...)` + `when(() => mockChatRepository.saveSession(...)).thenAnswer(...)` + `when(() => mockChatRepository.sendMessage(...)).thenAnswer(...)` appears verbatim in 10+ tests. Extract a `_stubSendSession(repo, {streamController})` helper per AGENTS.md "Extract mock stub helpers" rule.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_sessions_controller_test.dart` lines 28–36: GetIt setup uses `if (!GetIt.instance.isRegistered<DomainLogger>()) { GetIt.instance.registerSingleton(...) }` — the conditional guard indicates shared mutable state between tests. Replace with `setUpTestGetIt()`.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_recorder_controller_test.dart`: `_ThrowingCancelSubscription`, `_ThrowOnCancelStream`, `_ThrowingDoubleCancelSubscription`, `_ThrowOnCancelDoubleStream` (lines 44–129) are value-less boilerplate. Consider extracting to a shared test-utilities file if the throwing-subscription pattern is used elsewhere.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `getNormalizedAmplitudeHistory()` in `chat_recorder_controller.dart` (lines 665–680) is a pure function mapping `List<double>` → `List<double>` with clamping. This is a perfect Glados candidate: property — for any dBFS input ∈ [−∞, −80], output must equal 0.05; for input ∈ [−10, ∞], output must equal 1.0; for any input, output ∈ [0.05, 1.0]; length-preserving.
- [ ] **[MED]** `ChatRecorderState.copyWith` — test that any field not passed is unchanged (round-trip / immutability property). The `useRealtimeMode` field is notably absent from round-trip checks in the existing static test.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `computeOpenTagCarry` already has static tests; a Glados property (`any.string` → `carry.length <= input.length`, carry is always a suffix of input, carry is never a complete opener) would catch edge cases in the priority-picking loop.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_stream_parser_test.dart` already has an excellent Glados property for chunk-invariance. No gaps here.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `stopAndTranscribe()` null `_filePath` branch (lines 317–328 of `chat_recorder_controller.dart`): the comment in the test at line 1557 admits the "null filePath" branch is hard to reach and was never properly covered. No test currently hits `errorType == noAudioFile`. This code path is reachable if `start()` sets `_recorder` but `_filePath` is null (e.g., a race where the controller is rebuilt between `recorder.start()` and setting `_filePath`). Add a targeted test that directly sets `_recorder` without `_filePath` and calls `stopAndTranscribe`.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** `stopRealtime()` error path (lines 597–609 of `chat_recorder_controller.dart`): when `_realtimeService.stop(...)` throws, the controller calls `_realtimeService.dispose()` inside the catch. There is no test verifying that (a) `dispose()` is called on the service and (b) `errorType == transcriptionFailed` is set in the state.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_session_controller.dart` `retryLastMessage()` when `messages` are all assistant messages (no user message): returns without sending. Not tested.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_session_controller.dart` `deleteSession()` with empty ID: the guard `if (state.id.isEmpty) return;` is tested, but the branch where `clearChat()` itself throws during `deleteSession` recovery is not.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_sessions_controller.dart` error path of `switchToSession` when `getSession` throws (not returns null): only the null case is tested; the thrown-exception path that sets `error` is absent.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `ChatRecorderConfig` constructor with non-default values — its three fields (`sampleRate`, `maxSeconds`, `amplitudeIntervalMs`) are only used via defaults; no test exercises non-default construction to verify the values flow into the recorder.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `chat_recorder_controller_test.dart` multiple tests use `Stream.periodic(const Duration(milliseconds: 50), ...).take(5)` for amplitude emission, introducing 5 × 50 ms = 250 ms real time per test. Replace with `Stream.fromIterable([...])` (synchronous, zero real time), which is the pattern used in other recorder tests in the same file.
- [ ] **[HIGH]** The "pump 10 times" polling loops (lines 413–416, 962–965) that drive the unawaited `onDispose` cleanup chain are slow and flaky. Each `pumpEventQueue()` call has an undefined cost. A deterministic cleanup hook would eliminate these loops entirely.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_sessions_controller_test.dart` line 64: `Future.delayed(10ms)` is a real sleep between assertions. Replacing with `pumpEventQueue()` would eliminate the wait and make the test deterministic.
- [ ] **[MED]** `chat_session_controller_test.dart` streaming tests: each test creates a new `ProviderContainer` with `saveSession` stubs and a `StreamController`. The boilerplate is ~30 lines per test. Extracting a `_TestBench` class per AGENTS.md with `create()` / `sendMessage()` / `emitChunk()` / `close()` helpers would reduce per-test setup and allow `setUpAll` or `setUp` reuse, cutting total build time.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `chat_recorder_controller_test.dart`: several tests create a real `Directory.systemTemp.createTemp(...)` per test and clean up on teardown. This is real I/O and contributes to test latency on CI. Grouping tests that share the same temp-dir lifecycle into a `setUp`/`tearDown` pair would cut directory creation overhead.

---

## Summary

- `chat_recorder_controller.dart` at 806 lines is the most oversized file; primary split seam is batch vs. realtime recording paths (~400 + ~250 lines each).
- 4 inline mock class definitions in `chat_recorder_controller_test.dart` violate the centralized-mocks rule; `setUpTestGetIt()` is not used in any controller test.
- `chat_sessions_controller_test.dart` contains a `Future.delayed(10ms)` real-time wait; `chat_session_controller_test.dart` has 10+ `Future.delayed(Duration.zero)` calls that should become `pumpEventQueue()` or `fakeAsync`.
- `getNormalizedAmplitudeHistory()` is the best Glados candidate: pure, bounded, testable as a range/clamp property.
- Two significant coverage gaps: the `noAudioFile` error path in `stopAndTranscribe()` and the `stopRealtime()` failure → `service.dispose()` path.
- 5 speed wins: `Stream.periodic` → `Stream.fromIterable` (250 ms/test), polling `pumpEventQueue` loops, per-test temp directory creation, `Future.delayed` replacements, and streaming-test boilerplate extraction into a `_TestBench`.
